arguments.task: Research transcriptapi.com for official evidence about its API: find developer/API docs, authentication method, pricing or access requirements, rate limits, changelog or support notes, and whether there is any official or GitHub-linked MCP server. Open relevant first-party pages and extract the URLs and exact facts needed for a structured app research record. Prefer official docs, pricing pages, support pages, GitHub pages linked by the vendor, and MCP registry listings if linked from official/GitHub sources.

raw_result.description: API and MCP service for extracting YouTube transcripts and related video, channel, playlist, and search data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show Bearer API key auth for the REST API (Authorization: Bearer sk_...) and OAuth 2.1 support for MCP clients with Dynamic Client Registration and static registration options.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public docs and homepage pricing show a free self-serve tier with 100 credits/month; higher self-serve paid tiers are also listed.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST API under /api/v2 with endpoints for transcript extraction, video info, YouTube search, channel videos, and playlist videos; MCP offering exposes related tools for LLM clients.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include an MCP section, and the gathered evidence identifies an official GitHub MCP server at ZeroPointRepo/youtube-mcp with Smithery and Glama listings.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates 200 RPM on the free tier and 300 RPM on paid tiers; docs also note credits are consumed only on successful 200 OK requests.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs publish base URL, endpoint set, credit costs, and MCP auth guidance. Homepage pricing appears public, while billing management is login-gated. No dedicated public changelog was found; updates may instead be reflected through the GitHub repo history.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some findings rely on browser-run extraction summaries rather than directly quoted page text in-chat, especially pricing/rate-limit details and official status of the GitHub MCP repo; these are plausible from gathered evidence but should be spot-checked by a human.
